---
title: "Tic-Tac-Toe Gameplay with RewriteGames"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
jupyter: julia-1.11
execute:
  output: false
---

## Setup

This notebook assumes you have worked through `AlgebraicRewritingLab.qmd`,
which defines the Tic-Tac-Toe schema, board constructor, rules, and the
`x_won_check` / `X_sched` / `O_sched` schedules using the base
`AlgebraicRewriting` API.  Here we rebuild those same schedules using
`RewriteGames`'s `PlayerRuleApp` wrapper, which lets the wiring-diagram
schedule call back into agent code for move selection.

```{julia}
using Pkg
Pkg.activate(".")

using Catlab, AlgebraicRewriting
using RewriteGames
using Statistics: mean
using Catlab.Graphics.Graphviz: Attributes, Statement, Node
import Catlab.Graphics.Graphviz
```

## Schema and Board

We reuse the Tic-Tac-Toe schema verbatim from `AlgebraicRewritingLab.qmd`.

```{julia}
@present SchTTT(FreeSchema) begin
    Sq::Ob; E::Ob; X::Ob; O::Ob
    xsq::Hom(X, Sq); osq::Hom(O, Sq)
    src::Hom(E, Sq); tgt::Hom(E, Sq)
end

@acset_type TicTacToe(SchTTT, index=[:xsq, :osq], part_type=BitSetParts)
const TTT = TicTacToe
𝒞 = ACSetCategory(MADVarACSetCat(TTT()))

function create_board()
    ttt = TTT()
    add_parts!(ttt, :Sq, 9)
    for i in 0:2, j in 0:1
        add_part!(ttt, :E, src=3*i+j+1, tgt=3*i+j+2)
    end
    for i in 0:1, j in 0:2
        add_part!(ttt, :E, src=3*i+j+1, tgt=3*i+j+1+3)
    end
    return ttt
end
```

```{julia}
# Migration functor: swap X ↔ O
F = Migrate(
    𝒞,
    Dict(:X => :O, :O => :X, :Sq => :Sq, :E => :E),
    Dict(:xsq => :osq, :osq => :xsq, :src => :src, :tgt => :tgt),
    SchTTT, TTT)

# Yoneda representables and Names object
gSq, gE, gX, gO = ob_generators(FinCat(SchTTT))
yTTT = yoneda_cache(TTT; clear=true)
I  = TTT()
Sq = ob_map(yTTT, gSq)
E  = ob_map(yTTT, gE)
X  = ob_map(yTTT, gX)
O  = ob_map(yTTT, gO)
N  = Names(Dict("X" => X, "O" => O, "Sq" => Sq, "" => I, "I" => I))
```

## Rewrite Rules

### Mark-X rule

The `mark_x` rule places an X on any unmarked square.  Two NACs prevent
firing on an already-marked square.

```{julia}
id_Sq    = id[𝒞](Sq)
mark_X_l = id_Sq
mark_X_r = homomorphism(Sq, X; cat=𝒞)

mark_x = Rule(mark_X_l, mark_X_r;
              ac=[NAC(homomorphism(Sq, X; cat=𝒞)),
                  NAC(homomorphism(Sq, O; cat=𝒞))])
```

### Win-detection rules (no-op identity rules)

```{julia}
function x_win_pattern(indices::Vector{Int})
    pat = create_board()
    for i in indices
        add_part!(pat, :X, xsq=i)
    end
    return pat
end

win_indices = [[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9],[1,4,7],[2,5,8],[3,6,9],[1,5,9],[3,5,7]]
x_wins = [x_win_pattern(idx) for idx in win_indices]

# Structural patterns that match three-in-a-row via the board graph
row_col_structural = TTT()
add_parts!(row_col_structural, :Sq, 3)
add_part!(row_col_structural, :E, src=1, tgt=2)
add_part!(row_col_structural, :E, src=2, tgt=3)
for i in 1:3; add_part!(row_col_structural, :X, xsq=i); end

diag_structural_1 = TTT()
add_parts!(diag_structural_1, :Sq, 5)
add_part!(diag_structural_1, :E, src=1, tgt=2); add_part!(diag_structural_1, :E, src=2, tgt=3)
add_part!(diag_structural_1, :E, src=3, tgt=4); add_part!(diag_structural_1, :E, src=4, tgt=5)
for i in [1,3,5]; add_part!(diag_structural_1, :X, xsq=i); end

diag_structural_2 = TTT()
add_parts!(diag_structural_2, :Sq, 5)
add_part!(diag_structural_2, :E, src=1, tgt=2); add_part!(diag_structural_2, :E, src=3, tgt=2)
add_part!(diag_structural_2, :E, src=3, tgt=4); add_part!(diag_structural_2, :E, src=5, tgt=4)
for i in [1,3,5]; add_part!(diag_structural_2, :X, xsq=i); end

x_rows_rule  = Rule(id[𝒞](row_col_structural),  id[𝒞](row_col_structural))
x_diag1_rule = Rule(id[𝒞](diag_structural_1),   id[𝒞](diag_structural_1))
x_diag2_rule = Rule(id[𝒞](diag_structural_2),   id[𝒞](diag_structural_2))

x_rows_app  = RuleApp(:x_wins_rows,  x_rows_rule,  I; cat=𝒞)
x_diag1_app = RuleApp(:x_wins_diag1, x_diag1_rule, I; cat=𝒞)
x_diag2_app = RuleApp(:x_wins_diag2, x_diag2_rule, I; cat=𝒞)
```

## Building the GameSched Schedules

### Win-check sub-schedule

`x_won_check_gs` is a `GameSched` that succeeds (port 1) when X has three in
a row and fails (port 2) otherwise.  It contains only `RuleApp` boxes (no
player choices), so `run_game_sched!` will execute them deterministically.

```{julia}
x_won_check_gs = mk_game_sched((;), (init=:I,), N,
    (r=x_rows_app, d1=x_diag1_app, d2=x_diag2_app, mw=merge_wires(I)),
    quote
        won_r,  not_r  = r(init)
        won_d1, not_d1 = d1(not_r)
        won_d2, not_d2 = d2(not_d1)
        won12 = mw(won_r,  won_d1)
        won   = mw(won12,  won_d2)
        return won, not_d2
    end)
```

```{julia}
#| output: true
view_sched(x_won_check_gs; names=N)
```

### X's turn schedule

`mark_x_app` is a `PlayerRuleApp` tagged with player `:X`.  When
`run_game_sched!` reaches this box it enumerates all legal mark-X moves,
asks the `:X` agent to choose one, applies the rewrite, and records an
`Experience`.

```{julia}
mark_x_app = PlayerRuleApp(:mark_x, mark_x, I, :X; cat=𝒞)

X_sched_gs = mk_game_sched((;), (init=:I,), N,
    (mx=mark_x_app, wc=x_won_check_gs),
    quote
        moved, tie = mx(init)
        x_won, cont = wc(moved)
        return x_won, cont, tie
    end)
```

```{julia}
#| output: true
view_sched(X_sched_gs; names=N)
```

### Migrate to get O's schedule

Because every rule and interface ACSet inside `X_sched_gs` is stored
explicitly, the migration functor `F` (X ↔ O swap) can rebuild the entire
schedule for player `:O` in one call.  `player_migrate` also re-tags the
`PlayerRuleApp` box from `:X` to `:O`.

```{julia}
O_sched_gs = player_migrate(F, X_sched_gs, Dict(:X => :O))
```

```{julia}
#| output: true
view_sched(O_sched_gs; names=N)
```

### Full game loop

The outer schedule loops: X moves then O moves, repeating until either
player wins or the board is full.  The first return wire (`o_not`) feeds the
board back for the next iteration; the second return wire (`game_over`)
exits the loop.

```{julia}
game_sched = mk_game_sched(
    (trace_arg=:I,),
    (init=:I,),
    N,
    (x=X_sched_gs, o=O_sched_gs, mw=merge_wires(I)),
    quote
        x_won, x_not, x_tie = x([init, trace_arg])
        o_won, o_not, o_tie  = o(x_not)
        game_over = mw(mw(x_won, o_won), mw(x_tie, o_tie))
        return o_not, game_over
    end)
```

```{julia}
#| output: true
view_sched(game_sched; names=N)
```

## Running the Game

### Define a terminal predicate and a Game metadata record

```{julia}
function ttt_terminal(W)
    # X wins?
    for rule in [x_rows_rule, x_diag1_rule, x_diag2_rule]
        !isempty(get_matches(rule, W)) && return (true, :X)
    end
    # O wins? (use F to get O-win rules)
    o_rows_rule  = F(x_rows_rule)
    o_diag1_rule = F(x_diag1_rule)
    o_diag2_rule = F(x_diag2_rule)
    for rule in [o_rows_rule, o_diag1_rule, o_diag2_rule]
        !isempty(get_matches(rule, W)) && return (true, :O)
    end
    # Draw — board full (9 X+O pieces on 9 squares)?
    nparts(W, :X) + nparts(W, :O) >= 9 && return (true, nothing)
    return (false, nothing)
end

game = Game(SchTTT;
    players  = [:X, :O],
    terminal = ttt_terminal,
    initial  = create_board)
```

### Random agents

```{julia}
agents = Dict{Symbol, AbstractAgent}(
    :X => FunctionAgent((state, actions) -> rand(actions)),
    :O => FunctionAgent((state, actions) -> rand(actions)),
)
```

### Play a single episode

```{julia}
#| output: true
exps = run_game_sched!(game_sched, game, agents; T_max=20)

println("Episode length : ", episode_length(exps))
println("Winner         : ", isempty(exps) ? "N/A" : exps[end].winner)
println("Done flag      : ", isempty(exps) ? false  : exps[end].done)
```

### Play 200 episodes and analyse

```{julia}
#| output: true
all_exps = [run_game_sched!(game_sched, game, agents; T_max=20) for _ in 1:200]

x_wins  = count(e -> !isempty(e) && e[end].winner === :X, all_exps)
o_wins  = count(e -> !isempty(e) && e[end].winner === :O, all_exps)
draws   = count(e -> !isempty(e) && e[end].winner === nothing && e[end].done, all_exps)
lengths = [episode_length(e) for e in all_exps]

println("X wins  : $x_wins / 200  ($(round(100x_wins/200; digits=1))%)")
println("O wins  : $o_wins / 200  ($(round(100o_wins/200; digits=1))%)")
println("Draws   : $draws / 200")
println("Mean episode length : ", round(mean(lengths); digits=2))
```

X goes first so it has a slight advantage in random play — expect roughly
55–60 % win rate for X, 30–35 % for O, and a small fraction of draws.

## Key Takeaways

| Concept | RewriteGames type |
|---------|-------------------|
| One player's rule box | `PlayerRuleApp(:name, rule, I, :player; cat=𝒞)` |
| Build schedule (with agents) | `mk_game_sched(trace_args, init_args, N, boxes, body)` |
| Visualise schedule | `view_sched(gs; names=N)` |
| Migrate X→O | `player_migrate(F, X_sched_gs, Dict(:X => :O))` |
| Run an episode | `run_game_sched!(sched, game, agents; T_max=20)` |
| Analyse results | `win_rate`, `episode_length`, `action_counts` |
